title: Photo oxidative degradation of metanil yellow by photo-Fenton reagent
author: Debarshan Mitra
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Chemistry, University of Calcutta, 92, Acharya Prafulla Chandra Road, 

 Kolkata-700 009, India 

  E-mail  : debarshan@scientist.com 

 National Institute of Research on Jute and Allied Fiber Technology (ICAR), 12, Regent Park, 

 Kolkata-700 040, India 

  Manuscript received online 23 July 2013, revised 21 October 2013, accepted 28 October 2013  

 At normal laboratory temperature and atmospheric pressure, experiment on waste water containing metanil yellow by photo-Fenton process were conducted to examine the effect of operating variables like the concentration of ferric ion, concentration of metanil yellow, pH, hydrogen peroxide and light intensity on the reaction rate. Metanil yellow was completely oxidized and degraded into CO 2  and H 2 O in this experiment. A mechanism for photochemical bleaching of metanil yellow by photo-Fenton’s reaction has been processed.
